working_on: Issue close-out campaign session S-C: health-check audit posture overhaul. Authors ADR 0057 (adversarial stance for project health-check audits), rewrites engine/operations/health-check.md and docs/health-checks/TEMPLATE.md to add user-buffered execution, freshness probes, posture inversion, and pushback/lesson drawer querying. Closes Issues #30 #28 #36.
outcome_summary: Closed Issues #30, #28, #36 with one new ADR (0057 adversarial stance for project health-check audits, sibling to ADR 0040) and major rewrites of engine/operations/health-check.md and docs/health-checks/TEMPLATE.md. ADR commits five contract elements: user-buffered execution (added per user pushback during plan-mode review — audit findings + guidance suggestions surfaced before any retire/convert/replace action; user adjudicates; downstream sessions execute via Issues per ADR 0048), posture inversion across Fit/Gaps/Infrastructure-without-function/Bloat with recommendation-shape dispositions, stats-as-proxy-for-function anti-pattern naming, ≥1 affirmative retire-candidate-recommendation requirement, cold-context probe per audit. Per-system freshness probes documented (MemPalace search-recall content quality, validator soft-warn acted-on rate, Supabase migration empirical postcondition, hook-log success-exit verification, register-content capture rate). New 'Accumulated pushbacks and lessons' section consumes the existing audit_mempalace() probes as canonical contract (no health_check.py code changes). TEMPLATE.md rewritten with new sections including a User-adjudication subsection left blank on arrival. First-exercise readiness note authored per ADR 0053; T1-A through T1-E pending the cadence-fired audit at S-0087. Handshakes: ADR 0040 See-also, ADR README index + count drift fix (17 → 23 engine), build_readiness/README.md class-5 entry, CLAUDE.md topical pointer. MemPalace decision drawer captured. Validator: 0 hard-fails, 381 soft-warns (366 missing_rigor_score Phase 5 baseline, 14 issue_collision expected, 1 adr_consequences_deliverable_audit pre-existing).
declared_scope: Resolve Issues #30, #28, #36: author engine/adr/0057-adversarial-stance-for-health-check-audits.md (sibling to ADR 0040) committing user-buffered execution + posture inversion + stats-as-proxy anti-pattern + ≥1-retire-candidate requirement + cold-context probe; rewrite engine/operations/health-check.md adding adversarial stance, freshness probes per external system (MemPalace/validator/Supabase/hooks/registries), adversarial posture-section rewrites, and an Accumulated-pushbacks-and-lessons section with concrete mempalace_search commands; rewrite docs/health-checks/TEMPLATE.md adding Freshness probes, adversarial posture prompt-questions, Accumulated pushbacks/lessons, Affirmative retire candidates, and User adjudication subsections; author engine/build_readiness/adversarial_stance_first_exercise.md per ADR 0053; light handshake edits to ADR 0040 See-also, engine/adr/README.md, engine/build_readiness/README.md, CLAUDE.md.

scope_delivery.explanation: Plan delivered all named scope: ADR 0057 (5 contract elements: user-buffered execution, posture inversion, stats-as-proxy anti-pattern, ≥1 retire-candidate-recommendation, cold-context probe), health-check.md rewrite (adversarial stance section + freshness probes per external system + adversarial posture rewrites + Accumulated-pushbacks-and-lessons section), TEMPLATE.md rewrite (new top-to-bottom shape with User-adjudication left blank on arrival), first-exercise readiness note per ADR 0053, all named handshakes (ADR 0040 See-also, ADR README index + count drift fix, build_readiness/README.md class-5 entry, CLAUDE.md topical pointer), MemPalace decision drawer captured. User-buffered execution principle was added to the contract per the user's pushback during plan-mode review ('the health checks adversarial stance needs to be buffered by reporting its findings and guidance suggestions to me before taking action') — captured in ADR 0057 as element 1, in health-check.md as a User-buffered-execution subsection, in TEMPLATE.md as the User-adjudication subsection left blank on arrival, and in disposition labels (recommendation-shape: recommend retire / recommend convert / recommend preserve-with-affirmative-case). Three Issues closed (#30, #28, #36) per the campaign plan. Out-of-scope items honored: no health_check.py code changes (the existing audit_mempalace() probes documented as canonical contract); no retroactive grandfathering (contract takes force at acceptance); no script-level cluster detection for pushback/lesson (deferred to follow-up Issue if S-0087 first exercise warrants it). Validator: 0 hard-fails, 381 soft-warns (steady at expected baseline; no new persistent categories introduced).

mempalace_activity.rollup_note: Manually merged at S-0085 close. scan_mempalace_activity.py was invoked three times during shutdown (intentionally — once before diary_write to verify state, once after, once after re-running search+diary_read to fix the second-rollup overwrite). Each invocation truncated current_mempalace.jsonl per Issue #37's fix, causing data loss across the multiple rollups within the same session. Cumulative session activity actually run via MCP: 2 search calls (boot + close re-run), 2 diary_read calls (boot + close re-run), 1 diary_write call (shutdown step 7), 1 add_drawer call (decision drawer for ADR 0057). The single-session multiple-rollup data-loss pattern is itself a candidate for follow-up Issue (extending #37's fix to handle within-session re-invocations via merge-not-overwrite); deferred.
